question: Which all-time Oscar record did 'Sinners' break at the 2026 nominations, and what first did its cinematographer achieve at the ceremony?
must_contain: Sinners set a new all-time record with 16 nominations (previous record 14, shared by three films), and cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw became the first woman ever to win Best Cinematography.
facts: Sinners earned a record 16 nominations at the 98th Academy Awards, surpassing 14
Autumn Durald Arkapaw was the first woman to win Best Cinematography
Sinners won 4 Oscars including Best Actor for Michael B. Jordan's dual role
hardness_note: The nomination record surfaces in one search; the cinematography first does not.
answer:
Here are the answers, grounded in the 2026 (98th) Academy Awards.

---

### All-time Oscar record broken by *Sinners* at the 2026 nominations

*Sinners* (directed by Ryan Coogler) received **16 nominations** for the 98th Academy Awards, breaking the all-time record for the most Oscar nominations earned by a single film in a single year. The previous record of 14 nominations was held jointly by *All About Eve* (1950), *Titanic* (1997), and *La La Land* (2016). At the ceremony on March 15, 2026, the film took home four Oscars [Screendaily](https://www.screendaily.com/news/oscars-2026-sinners-leads-with-all-time-record-16-nominations/5212899.article), [ABC7](https://abc7.com/post/sinners-cinematographer-autumn-durald-arkapaw-makes-oscars-history-1st-woman-win-category/18719260).

---

### First achieved by its cinematographer at the ceremony

Autumn Durald Arkapaw, the cinematographer of *Sinners*, won the Academy Award for Best Cinematography, making history as **the first woman** and **the first Black person** to win that category in the 98-year history of the Oscars. She was only the third woman ever nominated in the category (after Rachel Morrison, Ari Wegner, and Mandy Walker), and prior to winning she had already been the first woman of color nominated for the prize [Los Angeles Times](https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/awards/story/2026-03-15/oscars-2026-sinners-autumn-durald-arkapaw-first-woman-cinematography), [ABC7](https://abc7.com/post/sinners-cinematographer-autumn-durald-arkapaw-makes-oscars-history-1st-woman-win-category/18719260). During her acceptance speech, Arkapaw asked all the women in the audience to stand, saying, "I don't get here without you guys" [LA Times](https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/awards/story/2026-03-15/oscars-2026-sinners-autumn-durald-arkapaw-first-woman-cinematography).

Additionally, Arkapaw's work on *Sinners* made her the first woman to shoot on the large IMAX 65mm and Ultra Panavision formats [LA Times](https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/awards/story/2026-03-15/oscars-2026-sinners-autumn-durald-arkapaw-first-woman-cinematography).

question: What was the result of the opening match of the 2026 World Cup, and what disciplinary record did that match set?
must_contain: Mexico beat South Africa 2-0 at Estadio Azteca on June 11, 2026, in a match with three red cards — the most ever in a World Cup opening match.
facts: Mexico 2-0 South Africa, June 11, 2026, Estadio Azteca; scorers Julián Quiñones (9') and Raúl Jiménez
Three red cards were shown (two South Africa, one Mexico) — a record for a World Cup opener
It was Mexico's first ever win in a World Cup opening match (previously 5 losses, 2 draws)
hardness_note: The score surfaces in one search; the red-card record and first-opener-win angle do not.
answer:
## Result of the Opening Match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup

The opening match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup was played on **Thursday, June 11, 2026**, at the **Estadio Azteca** in Mexico City. Co-hosts **Mexico defeated South Africa 2–0** in front of a sell-out home crowd. The goals were scored by **Julián Quiñones** in the 9th minute (who also scored the very first goal of the tournament) and **Raúl Jiménez** in the 67th minute with a header. [Times of India](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/football/fifa-world-cup/more-red-cards-than-goals-mexicos-2-0-win-over-south-africa-becomes-first-world-cup-opener-with-three-dismissals/articleshow/131668768.cms)

## Disciplinary Record Set

The match set a historic disciplinary mark: **it was the first World Cup opening match ever to feature three red cards** — more red cards than goals. Brazilian referee **Wilton Sampaio** issued three straight red cards, making this the game with the most dismissals in any World Cup match since the infamous "Battle of Nuremberg" quarter-final between Portugal and the Netherlands in 2006 (which had four red cards). [The Athletic / New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7352942/2026/06/11/world-cup-mexico-south-africa-red-cards)

The three red cards were:

1. **Sphephelo Sithole (South Africa)** — 49th minute, straight red for denying a clear goalscoring opportunity by bringing down Brian Gutiérrez just outside the penalty area. Sithole became the first player since Bolivia's Marco Etcheverry (32 years earlier) to be sent off in a World Cup curtain-raiser.
2. **Themba Zwane (South Africa)** — 84th minute, straight red following a VAR review for violent conduct, after swiping Roberto Alvarado across the face in an off-the-ball incident. At age 36, Zwane became the second-oldest player ever sent off at a World Cup (after USA's Fernando Clavijo, 38, in 1994).
3. **César Montes (Mexico)** — deep into stoppage time, straight red for a professional foul (denying a goalscoring opportunity) after bringing down South African wing-back Khuliso Mudau on a counterattack.

**Additional disciplinary records set or matched:**
- **Most red cards in a World Cup opener**: 3, surpassing the previous record of 2 (set in 1990 when Cameroon's André Kana-Biyik and Benjamin Massing were both sent off against Argentina in Milan). [World Soccer Talk](https://worldsoccertalk.com/world-cup/south-africa-equal-unwanted-world-cup-record-in-defeat-to-mexico-with-two-red-cards-in-opener)
- **South Africa became only the second team in World Cup history** to have two players sent off in a tournament-opening match, matching Cameroon's unwanted feat from 1990. Unlike Cameroon, who famously won 1–0 with nine men against Argentina, South Africa lost decisively.
- The match ended with **Mexico fielding 10 men against South Africa's 9**.
- Despite the disciplinary chaos, the game saw only **23 fouls** — described as "clumsy challenges rather than cynical ones." [The Athletic / New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7352942/2026/06/11/world-cup-mexico-south-africa-red-cards)

question: What did Apple call the new Siri announced at WWDC 2026, and what is macOS 27's California codename?
must_contain: The new assistant is 'Siri AI' and macOS 27 is named 'Golden Gate'.
facts: Siri AI includes a dedicated chatbot app and can act on screen contents
hardness_note: Siri AI surfaces immediately; the macOS 27 codename does not (snippets confuse it with macOS 26 'Tahoe').
answer:
At **WWDC 2026**, Apple announced the long-anticipated overhaul of its virtual assistant under the name **Siri AI**. Multiple outlets covering the keynote report the same name: CNBC's rundown lists "Siri gets upgrades and a new name: Siri AI" [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/08/apple-wwdc-2026-live-updates.html); Lifehacker writes that "the long-awaited AI Siri [is] called, um, 'Siri AI'" [Lifehacker](https://lifehacker.com/tech/everything-apple-announced-at-wwdc-2026); and Mashable refers to it as "Siri AI," describing it as "a revamped version of the old Siri" with a standalone app and deep integration across the OS [Mashable](https://mashable.com/tech/macos-27-golden-gate-wwdc-2026).

For **macOS 27**, Apple gave it the California codename **Golden Gate** — i.e., **macOS 27 Golden Gate**. CNBC confirms "Next MacOS named 'Golden Gate'" [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/08/apple-wwdc-2026-live-updates.html); Mashable's article is headlined "Apple announces macOS 27 Golden Gate at WWDC" [Mashable](https://mashable.com/tech/macos-27-golden-gate-wwdc-2026); and the macOS Golden Gate Wikipedia entry states it "is the upcoming twenty-third major release of Apple's macOS operating system… announced on June 8, 2026, at WWDC 2026" [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MacOS_Golden_Gate). The new OS will be available in fall 2026 and runs exclusively on Apple silicon Macs.

question: Who is succeeding Tim Cook as Apple CEO, and what reasons did Cook give for why it was the right time to step down?
must_contain: John Ternus (SVP of Hardware Engineering) becomes CEO; Cook cited Apple's current performance, its product roadmap, and Ternus's readiness — and stays on as executive chairman.
facts: Announced April 20, 2026; Ternus takes over September 1, 2026
Cook becomes executive chairman; Arthur Levinson becomes lead independent director
Cook's three stated factors: company performance, product roadmap, successor readiness
hardness_note: The successor and new role surface in one search; Cook's stated reasoning requires his interviews.
answer:
Tim Cook is being succeeded as Apple CEO by **John Ternus**, who has been Apple's Senior Vice President of Hardware Engineering since 2021. Ternus, a 51-year-old mechanical engineer who joined Apple in 2001, will officially take over as CEO on **September 1, 2026**. Cook will remain at Apple as **executive chairman** of the board of directors. [NPR](https://www.npr.org/2026/04/20/g-s1-118159/apple-ceo-tim-cook-stepping-down), [BBC](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c1kr19lry18o), [ABC News](https://abcnews.com/US/apple-ceo-tim-cook-stepping-john-ternus-set/story?id=132217743)

### Reasons Cook gave for why it was the right time

In an all-hands meeting with Apple employees at Apple Park (first reported by Bloomberg and confirmed by multiple outlets), Cook explained that **three conditions had aligned simultaneously**, which together made the moment right to hand over the CEO role:

1. **The business is "doing great."**
2. **The product road map is "incredible."**
3. **John Ternus is "ready for the role."**

Cook told employees: *"These three things all intersected, and they intersect now. And so now was the time."* [Yahoo Finance](https://uk.finance.yahoo.com/news/tim-cook-explains-why-stepping-154434963.html), [The Apple Post](https://www.theapplepost.com/2026/04/21/70515/tim-cook-tells-apple-employees-he-plans-to-stay-for-a-long-time-after-stepping-down-as-ceo)

He also emphasised that he is **healthy**, that his **energy is high**, and that he expects to serve as executive chairman *"for a long time."* His new role will focus heavily on Apple's relationships with governments and policymakers around the world, an area where he has built relationships over more than a decade. [Yahoo Finance](https://uk.finance.yahoo.com/news/tim-cook-explains-why-stepping-154434963.html), [The Apple Post](https://www.theapplepost.com/2026/04/21/70515/tim-cook-tells-apple-employees-he-plans-to-stay-for-a-long-time-after-stepping-down-as-ceo)

In his public letter to the Apple community, Cook did not spell out a single "right time" rationale but described his deep gratitude for the role and his confidence in Ternus, calling him *"the perfect person for the job."* He will work alongside Ternus through the transition and stay on as executive chairman to help guide the company. [MacRumors](https://www.macrumors.com/2026/04/20/tim-cook-community-letter)

[Note: stopped early — time limit reached]

question: How many moons does Saturn have? Why do reputable sources give different numbers?
must_contain: There is no single stable number: 274 after the IAU recognized 128 new moons in March 2025, higher counts (~285) reported by 2026, older references say 83-146 — and the count depends on recognition date plus an unsettled definition of 'moon'.
facts: The IAU recognized 128 new Saturnian moons on March 11, 2025, bringing the total to 274
The new moons were found by stacking Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope images; most are retrograde, 1-20 km
Some 2026 sources cite ~285; Jupiter is second with ~95
hardness_note: Different counts surface in one search, but reconciling which is current and why (recognition dates, definition debate) requires reading.
answer:
Based on the information I gathered, here is the answer:

---

## How many moons does Saturn have?

As of the most recent official confirmation by the International Astronomical Union's Minor Planet Center (MPC) on **March 16, 2026**, Saturn has **285 confirmed moons**. This total was reached when the MPC announced the addition of 11 newly confirmed moons, bringing the count from 274 to 285 [IAU Minor Planet Center Confirms New Moons of Saturn and Jupiter](https://www.iau.org/IAU/IAU/News/Ann2026/MPC-New-Moons-Saturn-Jupiter.aspx). This is widely reported by multiple outlets that published the announcement, including EarthSky, which stated "On March 16, 2026, the Minor Planet Center announced an additional 11 moons for Saturn, bringing its total to a whopping 285" [More moons for Jupiter and Saturn! New totals here](https://earthsky.org/space/more-moons-for-jupiter-and-saturn-total-satellite-discoveries).

The 285 figure makes Saturn the planet with the most known moons in the solar system by a wide margin — nearly three times as many as Jupiter, which has 101 confirmed moons as of the same announcement [Space Daily](https://spacedaily.com/t-saturn-used-to-be-listed-with-83-moons-then-astronomers-looked-harder-found-dozens-more-and-by-2026-the-count-had-exploded-to-285-so-many-that-saturn-now-has-nearly-three-times-as-many-co).

Note that some sources — for example, the Solar System Wiki on Fandom — list a count of **293** as of June 2026 [Moons of Saturn - The Solar System Wiki](https://thesolarsystem.fandom.com/wiki/Moons_of_Saturn). This likely includes additional provisional objects not yet formally confirmed by the IAU Minor Planet Center.

---

## Why do reputable sources give different numbers?

There is no single "wrong" number — the differences arise from several well-understood causes:

### 1. Rapid, ongoing discoveries
Saturn's moon count has surged in just a few years:
- Before 2023: ~83 known moons
- May 2023: 64 new moons announced, total raised to **145**–**146** [BBC Future: Why Saturn's moons have been so hard to find](https://www.bbc.com/future/article/20230703-why-saturns-moons-have-remained-hidden-from-view)
- March 2025: 128 more moons announced, total reached **274** [NASA Space Place](https://spaceplace.nasa.gov/how-many-moons)
- March 2026: 11 more confirmed, total now **285** [IAU MPC announcement](https://www.iau.org/IAU/IAU/News/Ann2026/MPC-New-Moons-Saturn-Jupiter.aspx)

Different sources are updated at different times. For example, NASA's "Saturn Moons: Facts" page still reports 274 moons [Saturn Moons: Facts](https://science.nasa.gov/saturn/moons/facts) because it was published before the March 2026 update. Similarly, NASA's Space Place page says "As of March 2025, Saturn has 274 moons" [How Many Moons Does Each Planet Have?](https://spaceplace.nasa.gov/how-many-moons).

### 2. The IAU confirmation process takes time
The BBC explains that "simply observing a moon does not mean it is officially a moon." The Minor Planet Center requires multiple observations over several years to confirm an object's orbit around a planet rather than the Sun. Until that formal confirmation, objects are considered provisional candidates [BBC Future: Why Saturn's moons have been so hard to find](https://www.bbc.com/future/article/20230703-why-saturns-moons-have-remained-hidden-from-view). Some sources (like the Fandom wiki) may count these unconfirmed candidates, while others (like the IAU and NASA) only count officially confirmed moons.

### 3. What counts as a "moon"?
There is some ambiguity at the small end. The NASA Saturn facts page notes that some objects in the rings — such as a body unofficially called "Peggy" — "may be a moon forming or disintegrating, or it might not truly be a moon at all" [Saturn Moons: Facts](https://science.nasa.gov/saturn/moons/facts). Resolving whether a small ring particle is a distinct moon or just part of the ring system is not always straightforward.

### 4. The vast majority are very small, faint irregular moons
The newly discovered moons are tiny (about 1–3 km in diameter) and extremely faint (magnitudes of +25 to +27) — about 100 million times fainter than anything visible to the naked eye [EarthSky](https://earthsky.org/space/more-moons-for-jupiter-and-saturn-total-satellite-discoveries). They orbit far from Saturn and are difficult to detect. As telescopes and observational techniques improve — particularly the use of "shift stack" processing and the newly operational Vera C. Rubin Observatory — astronomers are finding them in large batches. This means the count will continue to rise, making any given figure a snapshot in time.

### 5. Naming conventions lag behind discovery
Of Saturn's 285 moons, only 64 have been given proper names (drawn from Gallic, Inuit, and Norse mythologies, per IAU rules). The rest are known only by provisional designations like "S/2023 S 51" [EarthSky](https://earthsky.org/space/more-moons-for-jupiter-and-saturn-total-satellite-discoveries). Sources that count only named moons will report a much lower number than those counting all confirmed moons.

---

**Bottom line:** The most authoritative current figure is **285 confirmed moons** (IAU Minor Planet Center, March 2026). The number varies between sources because the count is changing rapidly, different organizations update at different rates, the IAU confirmation process is lengthy, and there can be borderline cases about what qualifies as a moon.

[Note: stopped early — time limit reached]

question: What is the population of Nigeria, and why is the number contested?
must_contain: Official/UN-derived estimates put it around 230-240 million, but the figure is contested: Nigeria hasn't held a census since 2006, its own National Population Commission admits it lacks accurate figures, and credible critics argue the true number may be tens of millions lower.
facts: UN-based estimates are ~240 million (2026); the last census was 2006
The National Population Commission has publicly admitted it lacks accurate population figures
Skeptics estimate the real figure could be 170-180 million; a biometric census has been planned
hardness_note: One search returns a single estimate; the institutional story (census gap, NPC admission, overcounting incentives) requires more.
answer:
## Population of Nigeria

There is **no single agreed-upon figure** for Nigeria's population because the country has not conducted a national census since **2006**. That census recorded **140 million** people (provisionally; final figure 140.3 million). Since then, all numbers are projections or estimates, and they vary considerably:

| Source / Estimate | Figure | Year |
|---|---|---|
| Worldometer (UN data) | **242,431,832** | 2026 |
| Worldometer (UN data) | **232,679,478** | 2024 |
| Worldometer (UN data) | **227,882,945** | 2023 |
| UN projection | ~**237.5 million** | 2025 |
| World Population Review | **225,614,518** | 2023 |
| Macrotrends (midyear estimate) | **234,573,603** | 2025 |

The United Nations lists Nigeria as the **6th most populous country** in the world (after India, China, the US, Indonesia, and Pakistan). The UN medium-fertility variant projects Nigeria's population will reach roughly **359 million by 2050** and could surpass the United States around that time. The last official census — the 2006 count — put the total at **140.3 million**, with northern states accounting for about 75 million and southern states about 65 million. Kano (9.4 million) was the most populous state, followed by Lagos (9.0 million) — though Lagos state officials immediately disputed that figure, claiming their own parallel count showed **17.5 million**. [Worldometer Nigeria population](https://www.worldometers.info/world-population/nigeria-population) | [PRB 2006 census objections](https://www.prb.org/resource/objections-surface-over-nigerian-census-results) | [TruthNigeria](https://truthnigeria.com/2023/11/how-many-nigerians-in-nigeria-a-continuing-controversy) | [ICIR Nigeria](https://www.icirnigeria.org/nigeria-may-face-more-crises-if-its-population-surpasses-us-presidency-warns)

---

## Why the number is contested

### 1. No national census in nearly two decades
The last census was held in **2006** — more than 18 years ago. The United Nations recommends that every country conduct a census at least every ten years. Multiple attempts to hold a new census have been postponed or cancelled. A census slated for **May 2023** was postponed indefinitely, officially to avoid post-election tensions but also due to **lack of funding** and political will. Without a fresh enumeration, all current figures are projections that different organizations calculate using different assumptions, producing widely divergent numbers. [TruthNigeria](https://truthnigeria.com/2023/11/how-many-nigerians-in-nigeria-a-continuing-controversy) | [The Conversation](https://theconversation.com/nigerias-census-has-always-been-tricky-why-this-must-change-150391) | [ICIR Nigeria](https://www.icirnigeria.org/nigeria-may-face-more-crises-if-its-population-surpasses-us-presidency-warns)

### 2. Deeply politicized history of census-taking
Population figures in Nigeria are not neutral statistics — they directly determine **political representation** (parliamentary seat allocation), **federal revenue sharing** (the more people a state or region claims, the larger its share of national resources), and **civil service hiring quotas**. This creates a powerful incentive for every region — especially the **North** (largely Muslim) and the **South** (largely Christian) — to inflate its numbers. Since independence, every census has been embroiled in accusations of manipulation along north–south lines. [PRB](https://www.prb.org/resource/objections-surface-over-nigerian-census-results) | [The Conversation](https://theconversation.com/nigerias-census-has-always-been-tricky-why-this-must-change-150391) | [TruthNigeria](https://truthnigeria.com/2023/11/how-many-nigerians-in-nigeria-a-continuing-controversy)

### 3. A history of disputed censuses
- **1962 census**: The first post-independence count returned a provisional figure of **45.1 million**, which showed the southern regions combined outnumbering the North. The results were **cancelled** amid fierce controversy.
- **1963 census**: A recount under a newly created, politicized Census Board produced **55.7 million** — a jump of nearly 11 million — with the North now back in the majority. The results were widely rejected by southern leaders. This dispute is often cited as a factor that contributed to tensions leading to the **Nigerian Civil War (1967–1970)**.
- **1973 census**: Returned **79.8 million** with the North at **64.4%** of the total. The results were **discredited and never officially published**.
- **1991 census**: Organized under military rule by a non-partisan National Population Commission, it returned just **88.5 million** — far lower than projections based on previous inflated figures — and was considered relatively credible.
- **2006 census**: Plagued by political interference from design through implementation. The provisional figure of **140 million** was immediately contested. Lagos State Governor Bola Tinubu rejected the Lagos count of 9.0 million and said the real figure was **17.5 million**. Northern states endorsed the results; southern states largely rejected them. The pan-Igbo organization Ohanaeze Ndigbo said the census artificially reduced Igbos to a minority. [PRB](https://www.prb.org/resource/objections-surface-over-nigerian-census-results) | [The Conversation](https://theconversation.com/nigerias-census-has-always-been-tricky-why-this-must-change-150391) | [TruthNigeria](https://truthnigeria.com/2023/11/how-many-nigerians-in-nigeria-a-continuing-controversy)

### 4. Allegations of outright fabrication on the ground
Former census officials have described being **bribed or coerced** to pad numbers. In Nasarawa State, a former official named **Ezra Zagbayi** told TruthNigeria that in the Jenkwe local government area, a village of roughly 100 people had a reported population of **over 2,000** — names and ages were simply invented. Muslim northern households, which are often polygamous, were said to sometimes claim 60 children when they had only 7 or 8. During the **1991 census**, data from some southern states showed every single household reporting exactly **nine members** — the maximum that fit on the first page of the form — strongly suggesting systematic cheating at the local level. [TruthNigeria](https://truthnigeria.com/2023/11/how-many-nigerians-in-nigeria-a-continuing-controversy) | [PRB](https://www.prb.org/resource/objections-surface-over-nigerian-census-results)

### 5. Migration and porous borders
Nigeria is a signatory to the **ECOWAS free-movement protocol**, which allows unhindered entry from neighboring countries — especially **Niger, Chad, Cameroon, and Benin**. Migrants (including Hausa, Tuareg, Zarma, and Fulani groups) move into Nigeria, often settling in the **Middle Belt** (Plateau, Benue, Nasarawa, Kogi, Kwara states), and quickly blend into local populations. This undocumented migration further distorts any attempt to determine a reliable count and has fueled violent land-based conflicts in the Middle Belt. [TruthNigeria](https://truthnigeria.com/2023/11/how-many-nigerians-in-nigeria-a-continuing-controversy)

### 6. Logistical and financial obstacles
Even when political will exists, conducting a census in Nigeria is a massive logistical challenge. The country has **rough terrain**, areas only reachable by water, and a history of enumerators quitting over **unpaid wages** (thousands walked off the job during the 2006 census). In 2021, President Buhari approved **10 billion naira (~$26 million)** just for demarcating enumeration areas, but the census was still postponed. Many Nigerians suspect the government lacks the financial commitment or is unwilling to conduct the census because the current uncertainty serves certain political interests. [The Conversation](https://theconversation.com/nigerias-census-has-always-been-tricky-why-this-must-change-150391) | [TruthNigeria](https://truthnigeria.com/2023/11/how-many-nigerians-in-nigeria-a-continuing-controversy)

---

## Summary

Nigeria's population is estimated by the UN at roughly **237–242 million** (2025–2026), making it the world's 6th-most-populous country. But that number is **deeply contested** because:
- No official census has been conducted since **2006**, leaving a gap of over 18 years.
- Every census in Nigeria's history has been **politicized** along north–south, Muslim–Christian lines, with figures inflated to secure greater **federal revenue, parliamentary seats, and political dominance**.
- Former census officials have testified to **direct fabrication** of names and ages, especially in northern states.
- Different international organizations (UN, Worldometer, World Population Review, Macrotrends) produce projections that differ by **tens of millions**.
- **Undocumented migration** from ECOWAS neighbors adds another layer of uncertainty.
- The convergence of logistical failures, financial constraints, and political manipulation has made accurate population counting one of Nigeria's most intractable and sensitive issues.

[Note: stopped early — time limit reached]
